title: Psalms 150
subtitle: Let Everything That Has Breath Praise the LORD

heb.paragraph: "Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens. Praise him for his acts of power; praise him for his surpassing greatness" (vv.1–2). THE FINAL PSALM. THE PSALTER’S LAST WORD. The location: sanctuary + heavens (v.1). The reasons: power + greatness (v.2). The instruments begin: trumpet, harp, lyre (v.3). Everything is building toward the final verse.

notes.note: "Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens." Two locations: the temple on earth and the throne in heaven. Praise fills both simultaneously.
"Praise him with the sounding of the trumpet, praise him with the harp and lyre." The orchestra assembles. Trumpet: the call. Harp and lyre: the melody. More instruments follow.

sections.header: Verses 4–6 — “LET EVERYTHING THAT HAS BREATH PRAISE THE LORD”

heb.paragraph: "LET EVERYTHING THAT HAS BREATH (nəshāmâ) PRAISE THE LORD" (v.6). More instruments: timbrel and dancing, strings and pipe, resounding cymbals, clashing cymbals (vv.4–5). Every instrument the temple possessed is summoned. Then the final command — the Psalter’s last sentence: "Let everything that has breath praise the LORD." Not just Israel, not just the nations, not just nature — everything that BREATHES. The nəshāmâ is the breath God breathed into Adam (Gen 2:7). Every living thing exists because God breathed. Every breath is borrowed from God. The only appropriate use of that breath: praise. HALLELUJAH. The Psalter is complete.

notes.note: "Praise him with timbrel and dancing, praise him with strings and pipe, praise him with resounding cymbals, praise him with clashing cymbals." Six instruments + dancing: timbrel, dance, strings, pipe, soft cymbals, loud cymbals. The full orchestra. Every sound-maker in the temple.

"LET EVERYTHING THAT HAS BREATH PRAISE THE LORD. HALLELUJAH." The Psalter’s final word. The scope: everything breathing. The reason: God gave the breath. The response: return it as praise. The word: Hallelujah — the last word of the last psalm. The 150-psalm journey from "Blessed is the one" (Ps 1:1) to "Let everything praise" (Ps 150:6). From individual blessing to universal worship. From the tree by water to the breath of every creature. The Psalter is an arc from one person’s Torah-delight to all creation’s hallelujah.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: God is a key figure in Let Everything That Has Breath Praise the LORD.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVPraise him with the sounding of the trumpet, praise him with the harp and lyre,ESVPraise him with trumpet sound; praise him with lute and harp!NIVPraise him for his acts of power; praise him for his surpassing greatness.ESVPraise him for his mighty deeds; praise him according to his excellent greatness!

rec.text: ""Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens." Two locations: the temple on earth and the throne in heaven. Praise fills both simultaneously."

rows.label: The Orchestra — 150:1–3
rows.text: Sanctuary + heavens. Power + greatness. Trumpet, harp, lyre. The instruments gather.

themes.note: Worship peaks at 10: THE PSALTER’S FINALE. Every instrument + every breath = hallelujah. Mission at 9: everything that breathes. The arc: Psalm 1 (one blessed person) → Psalm 150 (all creation praising). The Psalter is complete.
